labels.clickToZoom: Click the image to zoom in

overview.description: PorcySys is a comprehensive swine farm management system that covers every aspect of your operation: pig inventory, reproduction, health, nutrition, warehouse, and financials.
paragraphs: The system is organized into modules accessible from the side menu. Each user only sees the modules permitted by their role.

Data flows automatically between modules: a farrowing creates a litter in Lactation, an insemination updates the sow's reproductive status, a warehouse receipt updates inventory. There is no need to duplicate records.
This manual covers all available features. If a module does not appear in your menu, ask your administrator about your role permissions.
tips: Use the sidebar search to quickly find any section of the manual.
Click any image to zoom in.
The system works best on screens 1280 px wide or larger.

initialSetup.description: When the system is blank, the Superadmin must complete an initial setup before farm managers can begin working.

steps.title: Step 1: Log in as Superadmin
steps.desc: Access the system with the Superadmin credentials provided by the support team. You will land on the main dashboard.

steps.title: Step 2: Configure global settings
steps.desc: Go to Global Configuration (gear icon in the header or in the menu). Set the company name, logo, currency, decimal places, time zone, and date format. Save your changes.

steps.title: Step 3: Register Farm Manager users
steps.desc: Go to Settings → Users and click '+ Register User'. Create one or more users with the Farm Manager role. The system will automatically send their login credentials by email.

steps.title: Step 4: Create farms
steps.desc: Go to Farms and click '+ New Farm'. Fill in each farm's details and assign the corresponding manager (must already be registered in the previous step).

steps.title: Step 5: Farm Manager configures their farm
steps.desc: The Farm Manager logs in and goes to Farm Configuration. Adjust the production thresholds (gestation days, lactation, nursery, grow-finish) to match their real-world operation. If left unchanged, system defaults apply.

steps.title: Step 6: Register products, suppliers, and additional users
steps.desc: Before production begins, the manager should register the warehouse product catalog, suppliers, and all other users (veterinarians, technicians, warehouse staff). The system is then ready to receive production data.

tips: Order matters: create users first, then farms. A manager without a user account cannot be assigned to a farm.
You can create multiple farms under the same Superadmin, each with its own manager.
initialSetup.warning: Do not skip Farm Configuration. The thresholds drive the system's automatic alerts (upcoming farrowing, weaning-ready, stage change). Wrong values mean meaningless alerts.

roles.description: PorcySys has 8 user roles. Each role determines which modules appear in the menu and what actions the user can perform.

navigation.description: The system uses a fixed side menu (sidebar) to navigate between modules.
paragraphs: The sidebar is organized into sections: Home, Administration, Operations, Health & Nutrition, Reports. Each section groups related modules. Only modules your role can access are shown.

In the top header you will find: the language selector (ES / EN / PT), the notifications icon, access to the AI Assistant, and the profile menu (user photo). From the profile menu you can access this manual, view your profile, and sign out.
The Dashboard (Home) is the main screen. It shows KPIs and summaries tailored to your role. Change the analysis period using the date selector at the top.
tips: On small screens the sidebar collapses. Use the hamburger icon (☰) to open it.
The open-book icon in the profile menu goes directly to this manual.

users.description: The Users module lets the Superadmin create and manage all system accounts. Each user has one or more roles and may be assigned to a farm.
paragraphs: Available actions in the users table: register a new user, edit details, activate/deactivate an account, and resend login credentials by email.
A deactivated user cannot log in, but their historical records are fully preserved. To permanently delete a user, contact the support team.

steps.title: Step 1: Open the form
steps.desc: Go to Settings → Users. Click '+ Register User'.

steps.title: Step 2: Profile photo (optional)
steps.desc: Upload a profile photo by clicking the image area. Accepted formats: JPG, PNG, WEBP. Maximum size: 2 MB.

steps.title: Step 3: Personal details
steps.desc: Enter first name, last name, and email address. The email must be valid and unique in the system — it is used to send login credentials.

steps.title: Step 4: Assign roles
steps.desc: Select one or more roles by clicking the role tags. Available roles depend on the creating administrator's own role. Multiple roles can be assigned to the same user.

steps.title: Step 5: Assign farm (if applicable)
steps.desc: If the role requires a farm assignment (Farm Manager, Veterinarian, etc.), select the farm from the dropdown. This field only appears when relevant to the selected role.

steps.title: Step 6: Save
steps.desc: Click Save. The system automatically generates a temporary password and sends the credentials to the user's email.

fields.name: Email address
fields.desc: Unique email in the system. Used for login credentials.

fields.name: Roles
fields.desc: One or more roles that determine accessible modules.

fields.name: Assigned farm
fields.desc: Farm the user belongs to. Required for farm-level roles.

fields.name: Status
fields.desc: Active / Inactive. Defaults to Active on creation.

tips: Assign only the necessary roles to each user (principle of least privilege).
If the user does not receive the credentials email, use the 'Resend credentials' button in the table.
You can edit user details at any time by clicking the edit icon.

farms.description: The Farms module (Superadmin only) lets you register and manage the farms in the system. Each farm has its own inventory, production cycles, and configuration.
prerequisites: At least one user with the Farm Manager role must exist before creating a farm.

steps.title: Step 1: Open the form
steps.desc: Go to Farms. Click '+ New Farm'.

steps.title: Step 2: Farm logo (optional)
steps.desc: Upload a representative logo or image for the farm.

steps.title: Step 3: Farm details
steps.desc: Fill in the name, code (auto-generated but editable), location, and status.

steps.title: Step 4: Assign a manager
steps.desc: Select the Farm Manager user who will administer this farm. If the user does not exist yet, you can create one directly from this form using the '+ New user' button.

steps.title: Step 5: Save
steps.desc: Click Save. The farm becomes active and the manager can log in to configure its parameters.

fields.name: Manager
fields.desc: User with Farm Manager role who administers the farm.

tips: Only the Superadmin can create, edit, or deactivate farms.
A farm can only have one assigned manager. To change managers, edit the farm.

configGlobal.description: Global Configuration lets the Superadmin set system-wide parameters that apply across all farms.

steps.title: Step 1: Access Global Configuration
steps.desc: Click the gear icon in the top header, or find it in the Administration section of the sidebar.

steps.title: Step 2: Configure company information
steps.desc: Enter the company name and upload the system logo. This appears in PDF reports and the application header.

steps.title: Step 3: Set currency and number format
steps.desc: Select the base currency, number of decimal places for amounts, and the date format used throughout the system.

steps.title: Step 4: Save
steps.desc: Click Save. Changes take effect immediately across the entire system.

fields.name: Company name
fields.desc: Name displayed in reports and the application header.

fields.name: Logo
fields.desc: Company logo shown in PDF reports.

fields.name: Currency
fields.desc: Base currency for all financial values.

fields.name: Decimal places
fields.desc: Number of decimal places for monetary amounts.

fields.name: Date format
fields.desc: Date display format used throughout the system.

tips: Currency cannot be changed once production data has been entered — plan ahead.
The logo appears on every generated PDF report.

configFarm.description: Farm Configuration lets the Farm Manager adjust the production thresholds and zootechnical parameters specific to their farm.

steps.title: Step 1: Access Farm Configuration
steps.desc: Log in as Farm Manager. In the sidebar, go to Settings → Farm Configuration.

steps.title: Step 2: Gestation parameters
steps.desc: Set the average gestation length (days), days before farrowing to start issuing alerts, and the maximum gestation days before triggering an overdue farrowing warning.

steps.title: Step 3: Lactation and weaning parameters
steps.desc: Set target lactation length (days) and the minimum age at weaning. The system uses these values to generate weaning-ready alerts.

steps.title: Step 4: Grow-finish parameters
steps.desc: Set target days and weights for nursery, grower, and finisher stages. These values populate stage-change alerts.

steps.title: Step 5: Save
steps.desc: Click Save. All automatic alerts and calculations update immediately.

tips: Configure these parameters before entering any production data.
Consult your farm's historical records or a veterinarian before changing gestation/lactation defaults.
configFarm.warning: Changing gestation length after insemination records exist will shift expected farrowing dates. Review any upcoming farrowing alerts after saving.

warehouseProducts.description: The Product Catalog defines all items that can be tracked in the warehouse: feed ingredients, medicines, vaccines, supplies, etc.

steps.title: Step 1: Open the form
steps.desc: Go to Warehouse → Products. Click '+ Add Product'.

steps.title: Step 2: Product image (optional)
steps.desc: Upload an optional product image to help staff identify it quickly.

steps.title: Step 3: Product details
steps.desc: Enter the product name, select its category (Feed, Medicine, Vaccine, Supply), unit of measure, reference price, and minimum stock level.

steps.title: Step 4: Save
steps.desc: Click Save. The product is now available for receipts, issues, and purchase orders.

fields.name: Reference price
fields.desc: Unit price used in financial reports.

fields.name: Minimum stock
fields.desc: Stock level that triggers a low-stock alert.

tips: Set the minimum stock level for critical products to receive low-stock alerts before running out.
The reference price is used for inventory valuation in financial reports.

warehouseSuppliers.description: The Suppliers register stores information about all vendors the farm works with. Suppliers are required to create purchase orders and record receipts.

steps.title: Step 1: Open the form
steps.desc: Go to Warehouse → Suppliers. Click '+ Add Supplier'.

steps.title: Step 2: Identification
steps.desc: Enter the supplier code (auto-generated, editable), supplier name, and supplier type (feed company, pharmacy, equipment supplier, etc.).

steps.title: Step 3: Contact information
steps.desc: Enter phone number, email address, and physical address. These fields are optional but recommended for easy purchase management.

steps.title: Step 4: Tax information (optional)
steps.desc: Enter tax ID (RNC/NIF) and any additional tax information if required for invoicing.

steps.title: Step 5: Save
steps.desc: Click Save. The supplier is now available to link to purchase orders and receipts.

fields.name: Name
fields.desc: Supplier's full name or company name.

fields.name: Supplier type
fields.desc: Category of supplier (feed, pharma, equipment, etc.).

tips: Fill in contact details to streamline purchase management.
Inactive suppliers are hidden from purchase order dropdowns but their history is preserved.

purchaseOrders.description: Purchase Orders let you manage the procurement process: create requests, track status, and receive products into inventory.
prerequisites: At least one supplier must exist.
At least one product must exist in the catalog.

steps.title: Step 1: Open the form
steps.desc: Go to Warehouse → Purchase Orders. Click '+ New Purchase Order'.

steps.title: Step 2: Select supplier
steps.desc: Choose the supplier from the dropdown. If the supplier is not listed, go to the Suppliers module to register them first.

steps.title: Step 3: Add products
steps.desc: Search for each product and enter the requested quantity and estimated unit price. Add as many products as needed.

steps.title: Step 4: Review and confirm
steps.desc: Review the order summary (total products, estimated total cost). Click Confirm to create the purchase order.

tips: A purchase order starts with status Pending. When products arrive, use the Warehouse Receipts module to record the receipt and update inventory.
You can add multiple products in a single purchase order.

warehouseIncomes.description: Warehouse Receipts record all incoming products, whether linked to a purchase order or entered directly.
prerequisites: At least one product must exist in the catalog.
At least one supplier must exist.

steps.title: Step 1: Open the form
steps.desc: Go to Warehouse → Receipts. Click '+ New Receipt'.

steps.title: Step 2: Select receipt mode
steps.desc: Choose 'With purchase order' (links to an existing PO) or 'Direct receipt' (no prior PO).

steps.title: Step 3: General information
steps.desc: Select the supplier, enter the receipt date, and optionally add invoice number and tax details.

steps.title: Step 4: Products received
steps.desc: For each product, enter the quantity received and the actual unit price. Add multiple products if needed.

steps.title: Step 5: Review and save
steps.desc: Review the receipt summary. Click Save. Inventory is updated automatically for each product.

fields.name: Mode
fields.desc: With purchase order or direct receipt.

tips: If the received quantity differs from the PO, enter the actual quantity — the system will flag the discrepancy.
Every receipt generates an automatic inventory movement in the Inventory module.

warehouseOutcomes.description: Warehouse Issues record all outgoing products from the main warehouse: direct consumption, write-offs, or manual adjustments.
prerequisites: Sufficient stock must be available for each product being issued.

steps.title: Step 1: Open the form
steps.desc: Go to Warehouse → Issues. Click '+ New Issue'.

steps.title: Step 2: Reason and description
steps.desc: The system assigns an auto-generated code and uses the current date. Select the reason for the issue (consumption, transfer, adjustment, loss) and add an optional description.

steps.title: Step 3: Products
steps.desc: Select each product and the quantity to issue. The system verifies available stock before confirming.

steps.title: Step 4: Confirm
steps.desc: Review the summary and click Save. Stock levels are reduced immediately.

fields.name: Reason
fields.desc: Reason for the issue (consumption, adjustment, transfer, loss).

tips: Issues automatically reduce stock. Make sure the reason is correct before saving — issues cannot be edited after saving.
For routine product consumption by health or feeding modules, stock is discounted automatically. Manual issues are for adjustments and corrections.

warehouseInventory.description: The Inventory module shows the current stock balance for every product in the main warehouse, along with movement history and low-stock alerts.
paragraphs: Stock levels update automatically whenever a receipt, issue, or transfer order is processed. You do not need to update inventory manually.
Filter by product, category, or date range to find specific movements. Use the export button to generate an Excel report of current stock.
tips: Rows highlighted in red indicate products below their configured minimum stock level.
Click a product row to see its full movement history.

subwarehouse.description: Sub-Warehouses are secondary storage locations within the farm (e.g., a medication room in the farrowing barn). Each sub-warehouse has its own independent stock.

steps.title: Step 1: Open the form
steps.desc: Go to Warehouse → Sub-Warehouses. Click '+ New Sub-Warehouse'.

steps.title: Step 2: Identification
steps.desc: Enter the code (auto-generated, editable), name, and type of sub-warehouse.

steps.title: Step 3: Assign a responsible user
steps.desc: Select the user who manages this sub-warehouse. They will be able to record issues from it.

steps.title: Step 4: Location
steps.desc: Enter the physical location within the farm (optional but recommended).

steps.title: Step 5: Save
steps.desc: Click Save. The sub-warehouse is ready to receive transfer orders from the main warehouse.

fields.name: Responsible
fields.desc: User responsible for managing this sub-warehouse.

tips: Products reach a sub-warehouse only via transfer orders from the main warehouse.
Sub-warehouse managers can record outgoing issues from their own location.

orders.description: Transfer Orders manage the movement of products from the main warehouse to sub-warehouses. The warehouse manager creates the order; the sub-warehouse manager receives and confirms it.

steps.title: Step 1: Create the transfer order
steps.desc: Go to Warehouse → Transfer Orders. Click '+ New Order'. Select the destination sub-warehouse and add the products with their quantities.

steps.title: Step 2: Confirm the order
steps.desc: Review the order and click Confirm. Status changes to Pending.

steps.title: Step 3: Sub-warehouse completes the order
steps.desc: The sub-warehouse manager opens the pending order and clicks Complete. They enter the quantities actually received. Stock transfers from the main warehouse to the sub-warehouse.

tips: Until the sub-warehouse confirms receipt, stock remains in the main warehouse.
A transfer order can only be completed by the user assigned as responsible for the destination sub-warehouse.

sales.description: The Sales module records the commercial sale of pigs — either individual animals or entire groups.
prerequisites: Pigs or groups must exist in inventory.

steps.title: Step 1: Open the form
steps.desc: Go to Sales. Click '+ New Sale'.

steps.title: Step 2: Sale mode
steps.desc: Choose 'By group' (sells an entire group) or 'Individual' (select specific pigs).

steps.title: Step 3: Select pigs
steps.desc: Select the group or individual pigs to be sold. Enter the sale weight per pig (or total weight for the group).

steps.title: Step 4: Buyer and pricing
steps.desc: Enter price per kg, buyer name, buyer type (individual/company/slaughterhouse), contact information, and payment method.

steps.title: Step 5: Review and confirm
steps.desc: Review total weight, total pigs, and sale value. Click Confirm. Pigs are removed from inventory.

fields.name: Price per kg
fields.desc: Price paid per kilogram of live weight.

fields.name: Sale weight
fields.desc: Live weight at time of sale.

fields.name: Payment method
fields.desc: Cash, bank transfer, check, or credit.

fields.name: Additional costs
fields.desc: Transport, logistics, or other costs deducted from revenue.

tips: The system automatically calculates total sale value from weight × price per kg.
Sales reports break down revenue by period, buyer, and animal type.

expenses.description: The Expenses module records all operational costs that are not product purchases: labor, services, maintenance, utilities, etc.

steps.title: Step 1: Open the form
steps.desc: Go to Finance → Expenses. Click '+ New Expense'.

steps.title: Step 2: Expense details
steps.desc: Select the date, category, and write a clear description. Enter the amount in the farm's configured currency.

steps.title: Step 3: Save
steps.desc: Click Save. The expense is recorded and automatically included in the financial reports for the period.

fields.name: Description
fields.desc: Clear description of what the expense was for.

fields.name: Amount
fields.desc: Cost in the farm's base currency.

tips: Use clear descriptions so financial reports are interpretable by auditors.
Expenses feed directly into the period's profitability analysis.

periodClosing.description: Period Closing consolidates the financial and production data for a given month. Once closed, data for that period is locked from editing.

steps.title: Step 1: Verify all entries are complete
steps.desc: Before closing, ensure all sales, expenses, and warehouse movements for the period have been entered.

steps.title: Step 2: Open Period Closing
steps.desc: Go to Finance → Period Closing. Select the period to close.

steps.title: Step 3: Review the summary
steps.desc: The system shows a summary: revenue, production costs, operating expenses, and net income. Verify the totals make sense.

steps.title: Step 4: Confirm closure
steps.desc: Click Close Period. The period is locked. Financial reports for that period become final.

tips: Closed periods can be re-opened by a Superadmin if corrections are needed.
Closing a period does not affect production data — only financial figures are locked.
periodClosing.warning: Once a period is closed, all figures for that period are locked. Make sure all records are complete before closing.

pigsView.description: The Pig Inventory shows all active pigs on the farm. Filter by production stage, sex, breed, ear tag, or entry date.
paragraphs: Click on any pig to access its complete profile: general info, medical history, feeding records, and reproductive events.
The inventory updates automatically when pigs are registered, transferred, sold, or culled. No manual adjustments are needed.
tips: Use filters to quickly locate specific animals.
The Details button on each row opens the pig's full profile.

pigRegister.description: Register new pigs individually (one animal at a time) or in a batch (multiple animals at once).

steps.title: Step 1: Choose registration mode
steps.desc: Go to Pigs → Inventory. Click '+ Register'. Select 'Individual' for a single pig or 'Batch' for multiple animals.

steps.title: Step 2: Individual — Pig details
steps.desc: Enter code/ear tag, date of birth, breed, origin (born on-farm or purchased), sex, current weight, and production stage.

steps.title: Step 3: Individual — Review and save
steps.desc: Review the pig's summary. Click Save. The pig appears immediately in the inventory.

steps.title: Step 2b: Batch — General batch data
steps.desc: For batch registration: enter the batch code, entry date, breed, origin, sex, and production stage that apply to all animals in the batch.

steps.title: Step 3b: Batch — Individual animal data
steps.desc: For each pig in the batch, enter the individual ear tag and weight. The system pre-fills shared fields.

fields.name: Stage
fields.desc: Current production stage (nursery, grower, finisher, replacement, breeder).

tips: For large batches, batch registration is significantly faster than individual entry.
Ear tags must be unique within the farm.

pigDiscard.description: The Cull Pig form removes an animal from the active inventory and records the reason (death, slaughter, sale, or productive cull).

steps.title: Step 1: Select the pig
steps.desc: From the pig inventory or the pig's detail page, click Discard / Cull.

steps.title: Step 2: Cull reason and details
steps.desc: Select the cull reason (death, slaughter, sale, cull), enter the date, weight at cull, and any relevant notes.

steps.title: Step 3: Review and confirm
steps.desc: Review the details. Click Confirm. The pig moves to the Culled Pigs module and is no longer shown in active inventory.

fields.name: Cull reason
fields.desc: Death, slaughter, sale, or productive cull.

fields.name: Weight at cull
fields.desc: Live weight recorded at time of cull.

tips: Culled pigs are never permanently deleted — they are visible in the Culled Pigs module.
Mortality data from culls feeds directly into production reports.

pigsDiscarded.description: The Culled Pigs view shows all animals removed from the active inventory along with the cull date, reason, weight, and responsible user.
tips: Use this view for mortality audits and production loss analysis.
You can filter by cull reason and date range.

groups.description: Groups (pens / batches) aggregate pigs by production stage. Each group tracks its own performance indicators: average weight, FCR, mortality rate.
paragraphs: The system provides specialized views per group type to streamline monitoring at each stage.
Operations available on a group: weigh, insert pigs, withdraw pigs, transfer to another stage, cull pigs in group, and apply health/feeding events.
tips: Groups allow collective management — one action (weigh, feed, medicate) applies to all animals in the group.
A pig can only belong to one active group at a time.

groupCreate.description: Create a group manually (select existing pigs) or in linked mode (system auto-generates the pig records).

steps.title: Step 1: Open the form
steps.desc: Go to Groups. Click '+ New Group'.

steps.title: Step 2: Group data
steps.desc: Enter group code, name, production stage, and entry date.

steps.title: Step 3: Add pigs
steps.desc: Standard mode: search and select existing pigs to add to the group. Linked mode: enter count, sex ratio, and average weight — the system auto-creates the pig records.

steps.title: Step 4: Review and save
steps.desc: Review group summary (total animals, average weight). Click Save.

fields.name: Pigs
fields.desc: Animals to include (standard mode) or count+weight (linked mode).

tips: Use linked mode for pigs coming from weaning — it is much faster than selecting individual ear tags.
Groups from weaning are created automatically when a litter is weaned.

groupOperations.description: Available operations on a group: weigh, insert pigs, withdraw pigs, transfer to another stage/group, cull pigs within the group, and change stage.
paragraphs: Weigh: records a collective weigh-in for all pigs in the group. Updates average weight and FCR calculations.
Insert: adds one or more pigs to an existing group (e.g., after purchase or transfer from another farm).
Withdraw: removes specific pigs from the group without culling them (e.g., for individual treatment or separation).
Transfer: moves the entire group (or a subset) to a different stage or pen.
Change stage: advances the group to the next production stage (nursery → grower → finisher → replacement).
Cull in group: records a cull event for one or more pigs within the group without affecting the others.
tips: All group operations are logged with date, user, and before/after values.
Bulk culls in a group update the mortality KPI on the group dashboard automatically.

replacementSows.description: Replacement gilts are females being developed for entry into the breeding herd. Track their growth, record weights, and promote them to breeder status when ready.

steps.title: Step 1: View replacement gilts
steps.desc: Go to Reproduction → Replacement → Gilts. The list shows all animals in replacement development.

steps.title: Step 2: Promote to breeder
steps.desc: When a gilt is ready for first service, click the Promote button. Enter the promotion date and any notes. The animal moves to the sow pool and becomes available for insemination.

tips: Gilts appear in Replacement after being registered with the 'Replacement' production stage.
Monitor weight and age before promoting — premature promotion affects reproductive performance.

replacementBoars.description: Replacement boars are males in development intended to become semen donors. Track their growth and enable them for the AI program when ready.

steps.title: Step 1: View replacement boars
steps.desc: Go to Reproduction → Replacement → Boars. The list shows all boars in replacement development.

steps.title: Step 2: Enable for AI program
steps.desc: When a boar passes quality evaluation, click Enable for Semen Collection. The boar becomes available in the Semen Collections module.

tips: A boar must be enabled before his semen collections can be recorded.
Run a first collection to evaluate quality before deciding on full program participation.

labExtractions.description: Semen Collections records every collection from a boar, including volume, motility, concentration, and overall quality evaluation.
prerequisites: At least one boar must be enabled for semen collection.

steps.title: Step 1: Open the form
steps.desc: Go to Lab → Semen Collections. Click '+ New Collection'.

steps.title: Step 2: Select boar and date
steps.desc: Select the donor boar from the active semen boar list. Enter the collection date and the responsible technician.

steps.title: Step 3: Quality data
steps.desc: Enter volume collected (mL), concentration, motility (%), and overall quality rating (Approved / Rejected). Add observations as needed.

steps.title: Step 4: Save
steps.desc: Click Save. Approved collections become available for dose preparation in the Semen Doses module.

tips: Only Approved collections can be used to prepare semen doses.
Record collections promptly to maintain accurate AI program inventory.

labSamples.description: Semen Doses records the preparation of insemination doses from a base semen collection, including dose count, storage method, and expiry date.
prerequisites: An approved semen collection must exist.

steps.title: Step 1: Open the form
steps.desc: Go to Lab → Semen Doses. Click '+ New Dose Batch'.

steps.title: Step 2: Select base collection
steps.desc: Select the approved semen collection from which the doses will be prepared.

steps.title: Step 3: Dose data
steps.desc: Enter number of doses prepared, expiry date, storage method (refrigerated or frozen), and storage location within the lab.

steps.title: Step 4: Save
steps.desc: Click Save. The doses are available for selection when recording inseminations.

fields.name: Base collection
fields.desc: Approved semen collection used to prepare doses.

fields.name: Number of doses
fields.desc: Total doses prepared from this collection.

fields.name: Storage location
fields.desc: Physical location in the lab (tank, shelf, etc.).

tips: Track expiry dates — expired doses cannot be used for inseminations.
Refrigerated doses typically last 3–5 days; frozen doses can last years.

gestationInseminations.description: The Inseminations module records each AI service performed on a sow, linking the female, the semen dose, the date, and the responsible technician.
prerequisites: At least one sow in available (non-pregnant, non-lactating) status must exist.
At least one semen dose batch must be available.

steps.title: Step 1: Open the form
steps.desc: Go to Reproduction → Inseminations. Click '+ Record Insemination'.

steps.title: Step 2: Select sow
steps.desc: Select the sow from the list of available females (non-pregnant, not in lactation).

steps.title: Step 3: Insemination info
steps.desc: Enter the insemination date and the responsible technician.

steps.title: Step 4: Semen doses
steps.desc: Select the semen dose batch(es) to use. Drag and drop to re-order if multiple doses are used. Enter the dose count.

steps.title: Step 5: Review and save
steps.desc: Review the insemination summary. Click Save. The sow's status updates to 'Inseminated'. Dose inventory is decremented automatically.

fields.name: Sow
fields.desc: Female to be inseminated (must be available).

fields.name: Semen dose
fields.desc: Dose batch to use (boar name + warehouse shown).

tips: Sow status updates automatically: Available → Inseminated → Pregnant → Farrowed.
Dose inventory is consumed automatically when an insemination is saved.

gestationPregnancies.description: The Pregnancies module shows all sows confirmed as pregnant, with expected farrowing date, days in gestation, and current status.
paragraphs: Pregnancy is confirmed by recording a pregnancy check (ultrasound or return check). Possible outcomes: pregnant, open (failed), questionable, resorption, abortion.
The system automatically generates alerts when a sow is nearing her expected farrowing date based on the farm's configured gestation length.

steps.title: Step 1: Record pregnancy diagnosis
steps.desc: From the sow's profile or the Pregnancies list, click Diagnose. Select the result (pregnant, open, etc.), enter the diagnosis date, and add any notes or attachments.

steps.title: Step 2: Monitor approaching farrowings
steps.desc: The Pregnancies list highlights sows close to farrowing. Use this to prepare farrowing stalls in advance.

tips: Open sows (failed insemination) should be re-inseminated promptly to maintain breeding efficiency.
Use the 'Upcoming Farrowings' widget on the dashboard to plan your farrowing week.

births.description: The Farrowing module records each farrowing event: live-born piglets, stillborns, mummies, birth weights, and farrowing type.
prerequisites: The sow must have a confirmed pregnancy in the system.

steps.title: Step 1: Open the form
steps.desc: Go to Reproduction → Farrowings, or click '+ Record Farrowing' from the Upcoming Farrowings section.

steps.title: Step 2: Select insemination
steps.desc: Select the insemination (and by extension the sow) this farrowing corresponds to.

steps.title: Step 3: Farrowing data
steps.desc: Enter farrowing date, farrowing type (normal, C-section, induced, dystocic, abortive), and attending staff.

steps.title: Step 4: Litter data
steps.desc: Enter live-born count, stillborns, mummies, total litter weight, and sex breakdown. The system creates the litter automatically in the Lactation module.

steps.title: Step 5: Review and save
steps.desc: Review the farrowing summary. Click Save. The sow's status updates and the litter appears in Lactation.

fields.name: Farrowing type
fields.desc: Normal, C-section, induced, dystocic, or abortive.

tips: Register farrowings promptly — the litter appears in Lactation only after the farrowing is saved.
Stillborn and mummy counts feed directly into reproductive KPIs.

lactation.description: The Lactation module manages litters from birth to weaning. Record piglet weights, pre-weaning mortality, fostering, and medication.
paragraphs: Litters are created automatically when a farrowing is recorded. The sow and her litter are tracked together throughout lactation.
When a litter is weaned, the piglets automatically move to Pig Inventory and are available for group allocation into a nursery pen.

steps.title: Step 1: View active litters
steps.desc: Go to Reproduction → Lactation. The list shows all active litters with their sow, piglet count, days in lactation, and current weight.

steps.title: Step 2: Record piglet events
steps.desc: From a litter's detail page, you can: record a group weigh-in, discard dead piglets (pre-weaning mortality), foster piglets between litters, or apply medication.

steps.title: Step 3: Wean the litter
steps.desc: When ready to wean, click Wean Litter. Enter weaning date and total piglet weight. Select a destination group (nursery) or leave blank to create a new group automatically.

tips: Record pre-weaning mortality promptly to keep piglet counts accurate.
The dashboard alerts you when litters have reached the target lactation length.

weaning.description: Nursery groups hold newly weaned piglets. Record collective weighing, feeding, and health events for the entire pen.
paragraphs: Nursery groups are created automatically when a litter is weaned (or manually when using linked group creation).
When the group reaches target weight or age, transfer them to the grower stage.
tips: Weigh nursery pigs weekly to track ADG and FCR accurately.
Nursery FCR target is typically 1.5–1.8 kg feed per kg gain.

growing.description: Grower and Finisher groups manage pigs from nursery exit through market weight. Track ADG, FCR, and cost per kg gained.
paragraphs: Grower/finisher groups receive pigs from the nursery stage via stage-change or transfer operations.
When animals reach market weight, they can be transferred directly to the Sales module.
tips: Record periodic weighings to keep FCR and ADG calculations up to date.
Finisher target weight varies by market contract — confirm with your buyer before setting the stage-change trigger.

medication.description: Medication Packages define pre-configured treatment protocols that can be applied to individual pigs, groups, or litters.
prerequisites: Medication or vaccine products must exist in the warehouse catalog.
The sub-warehouse where medicines are stored must exist (if using sub-warehouse stock).

steps.title: Step 1: Create a medication package
steps.desc: Go to Health → Medication Packages. Click '+ New Package'. Enter the code, name, and target production stage.

steps.title: Step 2: Add medicines
steps.desc: Select each medicine/vaccine from the product catalog. Enter dose, unit, frequency, and duration.

steps.title: Step 3: Save the package
steps.desc: Click Save. The package is now ready to be applied to animals.

steps.title: Step 4: Apply the package
steps.desc: From a pig's profile, a group, or a litter, click Apply Medication. Select the package and confirm. Stock is deducted automatically from the configured sub-warehouse.

fields.name: Target stage
fields.desc: Production stage the protocol applies to.

fields.name: Frequency
fields.desc: How often the medicine is given (e.g., once daily for 3 days).

tips: Packages allow one-click application of multi-drug protocols — much faster than entering each medicine individually.
Medication stock is automatically deducted when a package is applied.

vaccinationPlans.description: Vaccination Plans schedule recurring vaccine events for specific production stages — the system tracks due dates and completion.

steps.title: Step 1: Create a vaccination plan
steps.desc: Go to Health → Vaccination Plans. Click '+ New Plan'. Enter code, name, and target stage.

steps.title: Step 2: Define vaccines and schedule
steps.desc: Select each vaccine product, target age (in days), and application frequency.

steps.title: Step 3: Assign to pigs / groups
steps.desc: From a pig's profile or a group, click Apply Vaccination Plan and select the plan. The system schedules the upcoming vaccinations.

tips: Vaccination plan alerts appear on the dashboard when application dates arrive.
Keep vaccination records up to date for health certifications and traceability audits.

medicationAssign.description: Apply a medication package or individual medicine directly to a pig, group, or litter.

steps.title: Step 1: Navigate to the animal or group
steps.desc: Open the pig's detail page, the group detail, or the litter detail.

steps.title: Step 2: Click Apply Medication
steps.desc: Click the Apply Medication button. Select the medication package or choose a specific product.

steps.title: Step 3: Confirm
steps.desc: Confirm the application. The system records the event, deducts the product from sub-warehouse stock, and logs the responsible user and date.

tips: Medication events are visible in the animal's health history on its profile page.
If stock is insufficient, the system will warn you before saving.

feeding.description: Feed Packages define ration formulas specifying ingredients, percentages, and expected FCR for each production stage.

steps.title: Step 1: Create a feed package
steps.desc: Go to Nutrition → Feed Packages. Click '+ New Package'. Enter code, name, and target stage.

steps.title: Step 2: Define ingredients
steps.desc: Add each feed ingredient from the warehouse catalog. Enter the percentage composition and expected FCR.

steps.title: Step 3: Save
steps.desc: Click Save. The package is ready for use in feed preparations.

tips: Ingredient percentages must total 100%.
Feed packages link consumption to warehouse stock — ingredients are deducted when feed is administered.

feedPreparation.description: Feed Preparation records a physical batch of feed manufactured using a Feed Package formula.
prerequisites: The sub-warehouse where feed is produced/stored must exist.
Sufficient ingredient stock must be available.

steps.title: Step 1: Open the form
steps.desc: Go to Nutrition → Feed Preparation. Click '+ New Preparation'.

steps.title: Step 2: Select package and sub-warehouse
steps.desc: Select the feed package formula to use and the sub-warehouse where the batch will be stored.

steps.title: Step 3: Enter actual yield and ingredients
steps.desc: Enter the actual batch yield (kg), and for each ingredient enter the actual quantity used (may differ from the formula if adjustments were made).

steps.title: Step 4: Save
steps.desc: Click Save. Ingredient stock is deducted from the main warehouse. Feed stock is added to the destination sub-warehouse.

fields.name: Feed package
fields.desc: Formula to use for this batch.

fields.name: Sub-warehouse
fields.desc: Where the finished feed will be stored.

fields.name: Ingredient quantities
fields.desc: Actual amount of each ingredient used.

tips: Actual ingredient quantities are used for warehouse deductions — not the formula percentages.
Prepare feed before scheduling administration to ensure stock availability.

reportsProduction.description: Production Reports consolidate the farm's performance indicators: farrowing rate, litter size, pre-weaning mortality, ADG, and reproductive efficiency.
paragraphs: Filter by period, farm, and indicator type. Charts help identify trends and compare performance across periods.
Available production reports: Inseminations summary, Farrowing rate, Litter performance, Mortality by stage, Weight gain by group.
tips: Export reports to PDF for sharing with management or external advisors.
Compare back-to-back periods to identify seasonal trends.

reportsInventory.description: Inventory Reports show the complete warehouse movement history, period consumption, low-stock alerts, and inventory valuation.
paragraphs: Available reports: Inventory valuation (current stock × reference price), Movement history (all receipts and issues in a period), Consumption by product/category, Low-stock alert summary.
tips: Inventory valuation is based on reference prices set in the product catalog — keep these up to date.
Export to Excel for integration with external accounting systems.

reportsFinance.description: Financial Reports consolidate revenue, production costs, operating expenses, and net income per period.
paragraphs: Available reports: Income statement (revenue vs costs), Profitability by batch/group, Cash flow summary, Cost per kg produced.
All financial reports respect the period-closing lock — closed periods show finalized figures.
tips: Use the cost-per-kg report to benchmark your operation against industry averages.
Revenue figures come from Sales; costs come from warehouse movements and expenses.

reportsSales.description: Sales Reports show the history of pig sales: volumes, average prices, buyers, and period-over-period performance.
tips: Filter by buyer to analyze your most important commercial relationships.
Average price per kg trends help negotiate contracts with slaughterhouses.

reportsTraceability.description: Traceability lets you reconstruct the complete history of an individual pig or group: from entry to exit, through every recorded event.
tips: Use traceability for health audits, disease investigations, and certification requirements.
Every event (weight, medication, vaccination, transfer, insemination, farrowing) is included in the traceability trail.

reportsAudit.description: Audit Reports log every action taken in the system: record creation, edits, and deletions, with the responsible user and exact timestamp.
tips: Audit logs are immutable — they cannot be altered by any user.
Use audit reports to investigate data discrepancies or verify process compliance.
